reasoning: The FRED time series data shows recent positive momentum, with the spread rising from 8.88 on Nov 11 to 9.20 on Nov 21. The statistical trend analysis from FRED projects a 62% likelihood of the spread exceeding current levels by Nov 30 due to short-term upward momentum (slope of +0.02 per day). However, financial time series like credit spreads predominantly follow a random walk with noise, meaning the medium- to long-term likelihood of an increase versus current absolute levels should converge toward 50%. Based on the principles of increasing uncertainty and reversion to typical spread levels in the longer run, I assign a 55% probability for the short-term window (when current momentum is strongest), decaying gradually back toward a symmetric 50% for the 1-month and 3-month horizons.
